proxy.label: Proxy
proxy.tooltip: Route requests through server-side proxy to bypass CORS restrictions

response.emptyState: Enter a URL and click Send to get started
response.largeResponseWarning: Response is larger than 1MB. Showing first 100KB.
response.cookieNote: Cookies are only visible for same-origin requests or when the server exposes Set-Cookie via CORS headers.
response.timingNote: Detailed timing (TTFB, Download) is only available when the server returns Timing-Allow-Origin header.
response.redirectNote: Browser fetch does not expose intermediate redirect URLs. Only the final destination is shown.

error.cors: CORS policy blocked this request. The target API does not allow cross-origin requests from browsers.

descriptions.text: Send HTTP requests and test REST APIs directly in your browser. Supports all common methods with headers, body, and authentication. Look up status codes in the [HTTP Status](/httpstatus) reference, or encode query parameters with the [URL Encoder](/urlencoder).

features.title: Features
features.methods: All standard HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS
features.body: Request body formats: JSON, Form-Data, x-www-form-urlencoded, Raw text
features.response: Response inspection: formatted body, headers, cookies, timing, redirect info
features.history: Request history with one-click restore (stored locally)
features.proxy: Server-side proxy mode: bypass CORS restrictions with a single toggle

cors.title: CORS & Proxy
cors.text: By default, this tool uses the browser's native fetch() API. Cross-origin requests require the target server to include appropriate CORS headers. If a server does not support CORS, enable the Proxy toggle — requests will be routed through the server-side proxy, bypassing CORS restrictions entirely.

descriptions.aeoDefinition: HTTP Client is a free online tool for sending HTTP requests and testing REST APIs directly in your browser. Supports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE with headers, body, and auth. All requests run locally by default; optional server-side proxy for CORS bypass.
descriptions.whatIs: A browser-based HTTP client for testing REST APIs. Send GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, and OPTIONS requests with custom headers, body, and authentication — all from your browser.
descriptions.stepsTitle: How to Send an HTTP Request
descriptions.step1Text: Type or paste the API endpoint URL. Select an HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, etc.).
descriptions.step2Text: Add query parameters, headers, request body (JSON, form-data, or raw), and authentication as needed.
descriptions.step3Text: Click Send. View the response body, headers, cookies, timing, and redirect info in the response panel.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Test REST API endpoints during development without installing desktop tools like Postman or curl.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Inspect response headers, status codes, and timing to diagnose API issues. Use the proxy toggle to bypass CORS.
descriptions.faq1Q: What is the HTTP Client tool?
descriptions.faq1A: A browser-based HTTP client for testing APIs. Send GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, and OPTIONS requests with custom headers, query parameters, and request bodies.
descriptions.faq2A: Yes. Supports Basic Auth, Bearer tokens, API keys (header and query), and custom authentication headers. All credentials stay in your browser.
descriptions.faq3A: Yes. All requests are saved to local storage with full request/response details. You can bookmark frequently used requests for quick access.
